payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines the roles, relationships, and reciprocal responsibilities of family members within the household economy, establishing who may participate and the nature of their accountability to one another.

payload.name: Earning Entitlement
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to value-based compensation, defining what constitutes earnable value, the structures through which it is earned, and the child's right to claim what has been earned.

payload.name: Performance Transparency
payload.description: Governs the enterprise obligation to provide visibility into opportunity, achievement, and outcomes, ensuring participants can see what is available, track their progress, and understand their standing.

payload.name: Parental Accountability
payload.description: Establishes parental responsibility for oversight, financial execution, and governance of the household earning system, including the obligation to monitor, report, and discharge payment commitments.

payload.name: Record Retention Obligation
payload.description: Defines the enterprise commitment to maintain historical records over time for auditability, pattern analysis, and long-term accountability across all earning and payment activities.

payload.name: Task Obligation Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and making available discrete obligations that carry monetary value, governing what work is offered within the household economy and how such obligations are structured and presented to participants.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing how participants claim and fulfill obligations, establishing accountability for completion and linking performed work to the individual who executed it, ensuring proper attribution of effort within the household economy.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and presenting the financial position resulting from completed obligations, aggregating value earned within defined cycles, and providing visibility to both earners and those responsible for disbursement to enable settlement transactions.

payload.name: Performance Record Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations across time, ensuring historical fulfillment data remains accessible for review, audit, and long-term accountability purposes beyond immediate settlement cycles.

payload.name: Operational Cycle Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing temporal boundaries that govern when obligations refresh, when settlement periods conclude, and how the household economy transitions between operational states to maintain rhythm and predictability.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle Model
payload.description: Logical structure and state transitions governing task definitions from creation through availability, claiming, and completion, including temporal state reset behavior at weekly boundaries.

payload.name: Task Completion Attribution Model
payload.description: Logical structure linking task completions to specific child entities, enforcing single-child association constraints and maintaining historical completion records for audit and reporting purposes.

payload.name: Earnings Derivation Logic
payload.description: Logical process for deriving weekly monetary aggregates per child based on completed task monetary values, including temporal aggregation boundaries and accumulation rules.

payload.name: Parent Supervisory Visibility Model
payload.description: Logical role-based access model enabling parent actors to view aggregated completion and earnings data across children, create task definitions, and access reporting functions within their supervisory scope.

payload.name: Child Task Interaction Model
payload.description: Logical role and filtering rules governing child actor access to task subsets based on availability and completion status, including task claiming authorization and earnings visibility within temporal scope.

payload.name: Operating System Platform
payload.description: Physical deployment target and compatibility constraints governing the operating system platform on which the system will be built and deployed.

payload.name: Task Data Specification
payload.description: Detailed specification of task operational data structures including status indicators, assignment relationships, and monetary value field definitions required for task lifecycle management.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle Process Specification
payload.description: Detailed algorithmic specifications for task claiming procedures, task definition workflows, and monetary value assignment operations executed by actor roles.

payload.name: User Interface Actor Specification
payload.description: Detailed interface specifications defining child and parent actor interactions, operational access patterns, and role-specific UI component configurations for task management and payment operations.

payload.name: Operational Cycle Timing Specification
payload.description: Detailed timing specifications defining weekly cycle parameters, endpoint event sequencing, reset timing constraints, and state transition scheduling for task availability and earnings calculation.

payload.name: Data Access Constraint Specification
payload.description: Detailed implementation-level constraints specifying data retention requirements beyond cycle boundaries and actor-specific visibility scope limitations for operational task information.

payload.statement: The task_completion table shall define a child_id column of type INTEGER with NOT NULL constraint serving as a foreign key attribute.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact column name, data type, and nullability constraint for the child entity identifier foreign key attribute.
payload.fit_criteria: Column child_id exists with type INTEGER NOT NULL.

payload.statement: The task table shall define a monetary_value column of type DECIMAL(10,2) with NOT NULL constraint and CHECK constraint (monetary_value >= 0).
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact SQL data type with precision and scale, nullability, and non-negative CHECK constraint for the monetary value attribute.
payload.fit_criteria: Column monetary_value has type DECIMAL(10,2) with CHECK constraint enforcing non-negative values.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task records in a relational table named 'task' with columns task_id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT), description (VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL), monetary_value (DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL), status (VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL), and assigned_child_id (INTEGER NULL).
payload.rationale: Defines the exact table schema, column specifications, data types, and constraints for task definition persistence.
payload.fit_criteria: Table 'task' exists with specified schema and supports 'available' as a valid status enumeration value.

payload.statement: The system shall create index idx_task_status on the status column of the task table.
payload.rationale: Specifies the index creation obligation for performance optimization of status-based queries.
payload.fit_criteria: Index idx_task_status exists on task.status column.

payload.statement: The parent_task_definition table shall include columns parent_id (foreign key to parent entity) and task_definition_id (foreign key to task_definition entity) with a composite primary key.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact relational table structure and key constraints for linking parent identifiers to created task definitions.

payload.statement: The task_completion table shall include columns task_completion_id (primary key), task_id (foreign key), child_id (foreign key), completion_timestamp (datetime), and monetary_value (decimal(10,2)), with no deletion or archival operations permitted.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact relational schema for task completion records with indefinite retention enforced through absence of deletion operations.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that DELETE operations on the task_completion table are disabled at the database permission level and that the query API exposes only SELECT operations on historical task completion data.
payload.rationale: Specifies the enforcement mechanism for persistent storage and constrained access through database-level permission configuration.

payload.statement: The task_definition table shall include columns task_name (varchar(255)), task_description (text), and monetary_value (decimal(10,2) with CHECK constraint >= 0).
payload.rationale: Specifies exact column names, data types, precision, and non-negative constraint for task entity descriptive attributes and monetary value.

payload.statement: The task_instance table shall include column status (varchar(20)) with CHECK constraint IN ('available', 'completed').
payload.rationale: Defines the exact enumeration constraint for task instance state attribute supporting only 'available' and 'completed' values.

payload.statement: The system shall execute INSERT INTO task_definition (task_name, task_description, monetary_value, parent_id, created_timestamp) VALUES (...) upon receiving POST /api/parent/{parent_id}/tasks API request with valid JSON payload.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact SQL insertion operation and API trigger for persisting task definition records from parent-initiated creation requests.

payload.statement: The system shall invoke EarningsCalculationService.calculateWeeklyEarnings(child_id, completion_timestamp) method when processing a TaskCompletedEvent message.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact service method invocation and parameters for earnings calculation triggered by task completion events.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that UPDATE task_instance SET status = 'available' operations occur exclusively via the WeeklyCycleResetJob scheduled process executing at 00:00:00 UTC each Monday.
payload.rationale: Defines the precise enforcement mechanism, scheduled job name, and execution timing for state transitions to 'available' at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Execution timestamp modulo 604800 seconds equals 0 at UTC Monday midnight

payload.statement: The child_entity table shall include columns child_id (primary key, uuid), child_name (varchar(255)), and foreign key relationships via task_completion.child_id and child_weekly_earnings.child_id.
payload.rationale: Specifies exact schema columns, data types, primary key, and foreign key relationships linking child entities to task completions and earnings records.

payload.statement: The system shall expose API endpoint GET /api/tasks/completions with query parameters start_date, end_date, child_id returning JSON array of task_completion records from the task_completion table.
payload.rationale: Defines exact API endpoint path, query parameters, and response structure for querying historical task completion data from persistent storage.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a UNIQUE NOT NULL foreign key constraint on task_completion.child_id referencing child_entity.child_id.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact database constraint type and referential integrity rule ensuring each task completion references exactly one child entity.

payload.statement: The weekly_earnings table shall include columns child_id (UUID, NOT NULL), week_start_timestamp (TIMESTAMPTZ, NOT NULL), and earnings_total (DECIMAL(10,2), NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00).
payload.rationale: Specifies precise field definitions, data types, and nullability constraints for the weekly earnings aggregate schema derived from Row 4 structural obligation.

payload.statement: The task_definitions table shall include a reward_value column of type DECIMAL(10,2) with a NOT NULL constraint and a CHECK constraint requiring reward_value >= 0.00.
payload.rationale: Defines exact data type, nullability, and validation constraint for the monetary value attribute in the task definition schema.

payload.statement: The parent_child_supervision table shall include columns parent_id (UUID, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES parents(id)), child_id (UUID, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES children(id)), and a composite primary key on (parent_id, child_id).
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact relational schema structure, foreign key constraints, and primary key definition for the parent-child supervisory relationship table.

payload.statement: The task_ownership table shall include columns task_id (UUID, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES task_definitions(id)), created_by_parent_id (UUID, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES parents(id)), and a primary key on task_id.
payload.rationale: Defines the precise relational schema for linking task definitions to the parent actors who created them.

payload.statement: The parent_reports view shall include columns parent_id (UUID), child_id (UUID), tasks_completed (INTEGER), and total_earnings (DECIMAL(10,2)).
payload.rationale: Defines exact field names, data types, and composition for the parent report schema structure.

payload.statement: The child_earnings_state table shall include columns child_id (UUID, NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES children(id) ON DELETE CASCADE), current_week_earnings (DECIMAL(10,2), NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00), and last_updated_timestamp (TIMESTAMPTZ, NOT NULL).
payload.rationale: Specifies exact field definitions, data types, foreign key constraint, and cascade behavior for the child-earnings relationship table.

payload.statement: The task_completions table shall include a completed_by_child_id column of type UUID with a NOT NULL constraint and a FOREIGN KEY constraint REFERENCES children(id).
payload.rationale: Defines the exact data type, nullability, and foreign key constraint specification for the child entity reference column in the task completion schema.

payload.statement: The database schema shall enforce a FOREIGN KEY constraint on task_completions.completed_by_child_id REFERENCES children(id) with ON DELETE RESTRICT to prevent orphaned completion records.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact referential integrity constraint definition and delete behavior to enforce valid child entity references.
payload.fit_criteria: Attempted deletion of a child record with existing completions returns constraint violation error

payload.statement: The weekly earnings aggregation service shall execute a SQL query that selects child_id and the sum of reward_value from task_completions grouped by child_id.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact query structure and aggregate calculation: SELECT child_id, SUM(reward_value) FROM task_completions GROUP BY child_id.

payload.statement: The weekly earnings aggregation service shall filter task completion records by completion_timestamp within a seven-day window starting at the current week truncation point (DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW())) and ending before the next week truncation point.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact temporal filter predicate using week-aligned boundaries: WHERE completion_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW()) AND completion_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW()) + INTERVAL '7 days'.

payload.statement: The weekly earnings aggregation service query shall complete execution in under 200ms for datasets containing up to 10,000 task completion records.
payload.rationale: Specifies the measurable performance threshold for the aggregation query execution.
payload.fit_criteria: Query execution completes in under 200ms for datasets up to 10,000 completion records

payload.statement: The system shall implement GET /api/child/{child_id}/weekly-earnings endpoint that returns a JSON response with schema {"child_id": "<UUID>", "week_start": "<ISO8601>", "earnings_total": "<decimal>"}.
payload.rationale: Specifies exact REST endpoint path and response schema format for retrieving weekly earnings aggregates.
payload.fit_criteria: Endpoint returns valid JSON schema with correct data types (child_id as UUID, week_start as ISO8601 timestamp, earnings_total as decimal); response time under 150ms at 95th percentile

payload.statement: The system shall return HTTP 200 status code when the GET /api/child/{child_id}/weekly-earnings endpoint successfully retrieves a weekly earnings record.
payload.rationale: Specifies HTTP success status code behavior for the weekly earnings endpoint.

payload.statement: The system shall return HTTP 404 status code when the GET /api/child/{child_id}/weekly-earnings endpoint finds no record for the requested child_id.
payload.rationale: Specifies HTTP not-found status code behavior for the weekly earnings endpoint.

payload.statement: The task_definitions table shall include a status column of type ENUM('available', 'claimed', 'completed') with a default value of 'available'.
payload.rationale: Specifies exact enumeration values and default for the task operational status attribute referenced in task lifecycle management.

payload.statement: The task definition workflow shall execute INSERT INTO task_definitions (id, created_by_parent_id, title, description, reward_value, status) VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), $1, $2, $3, $4, 'available') when invoked by parent actors via the POST /api/tasks endpoint.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact SQL INSERT algorithm and default field values for the task definition creation procedure.
payload.fit_criteria: Task creation completes in under 100ms with valid UUID generation

payload.statement: The GET /api/child/{child_id}/weekly-earnings endpoint shall return earnings data only when NOW() >= week_end_timestamp, calculated as week_start_timestamp + INTERVAL '7 days', returning HTTP 425 (Too Early) otherwise.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact timing constraint and HTTP response behavior enforcing that earnings totals become available only at cycle endpoint events.
payload.fit_criteria: Requests before cycle endpoint return HTTP 425; requests at or after cycle endpoint return HTTP 200 with data

payload.statement: The GET /api/child/{child_id}/tasks endpoint shall filter task_definitions using WHERE status = 'available' to limit visibility to available tasks.
payload.rationale: Specifies the status filtering component of the SQL constraint enforcing child actor operational visibility by excluding unavailable tasks.
payload.fit_criteria: Child actors retrieve only tasks with status 'available'; tasks with other status values are excluded from results

payload.statement: The GET /api/child/{child_id}/tasks endpoint shall filter task_definitions using WHERE created_by_parent_id IN (SELECT parent_id FROM parent_child_supervision WHERE child_id = $1) to limit visibility to tasks within supervisory scope.
payload.rationale: Specifies the supervisory scope filtering component of the SQL constraint enforcing child actor operational visibility by restricting access to tasks created by supervising parents only.
payload.fit_criteria: Child actors retrieve only tasks created by their supervising parents as determined by the parent_child_supervision relationship; tasks created by non-supervising parents are excluded from results

payload.statement: The Task operational data object shall include status, assignment, and monetary value attributes.
payload.rationale: Defines the composition of the Task operational data object with its required attributes.

payload.statement: The Task operational data object shall include a status attribute that indicates availability for claiming.
payload.rationale: Specifies the status attribute within the Task operational data object and its semantic purpose for claim availability tracking.

payload.statement: The system shall execute the task claiming procedure when invoked by a child actor role.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed operational procedure for task claiming by child actors at the implementation level.

payload.statement: The system shall execute the task definition workflow that assigns monetary values to tasks.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed operational procedure for task definition and monetary value assignment at the implementation level.

payload.statement: The system shall execute on the designated deployment platform locations.
payload.rationale: Defines the platform deployment constraint governing where the system must operationally execute.

payload.statement: The Child role shall grant operational visibility to task information access.
payload.rationale: Row 5 child role definition specifying operational visibility requirements as an access constraint governing task information.

payload.statement: The Parent role shall grant reporting access at operational cycle conclusion for payment fulfillment.
payload.rationale: Row 5 parent role definition specifying access rights constrained by operational cycle timing for payment reporting.

payload.statement: The Child user actor interface shall enable task claiming operations.
payload.rationale: Row 5 child actor specification defining operational task claiming capability.

payload.statement: The Child user actor interface shall enable earnings viewing operations.
payload.rationale: Row 5 child actor specification defining operational earnings access capability.

payload.statement: The Parent user actor interface shall enable task definition operations.
payload.rationale: Row 5 parent actor specification defining task creation capability.

payload.statement: The Parent user actor interface shall enable monetary assignment operations.
payload.rationale: Row 5 parent actor specification defining reward value assignment capability.

payload.statement: The Parent user actor interface shall enable payment operations.
payload.rationale: Row 5 parent actor specification defining payment execution capability.

payload.statement: The system shall calculate the earnings total aggregate at the weekly operational cycle endpoint.
payload.rationale: Defines when the earnings total aggregate data object is computed—at the cycle endpoint event when totals become available to child actors.

payload.statement: The earnings total aggregate shall consist of summed earnings values from the weekly operational cycle.
payload.rationale: Specifies the structural composition of the earnings total aggregate data object as a summation of cycle earnings.

payload.statement: The system shall execute the payment fulfillment operational procedure at the conclusion of the weekly operational cycle.
payload.rationale: Specifies the timing of payment fulfillment execution—triggered at cycle conclusion.

payload.statement: The system shall trigger an operational cycle reset event that returns tasks to available status at the start of each weekly operational cycle.
payload.rationale: Specifies the reset event behavior and its timing within the weekly cycle boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall persist operational data beyond the completion of the current operational cycle.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint specifying data retention policy that mandates persistence across cycle boundaries at the implementation level.

payload.statement: The system shall restrict child actor access to operational task information to only those tasks within the child actor's assigned visibility scope.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint defining actor-specific visibility limitation rule for operational task data access at the implementation level.

payload.statement: The calculation service shall aggregate task monetary values by summing all completed task reward fields where the task.child_id matches the target child entity identifier and the task.completion_timestamp falls within the specified seven-day window.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact aggregation algorithm for weekly earnings calculation from completed task monetary values.

payload.statement: The weekly earnings record shall contain fields child_id (foreign key), week_start_date (date), week_end_date (date), and total_earnings (decimal(10,2)).
payload.rationale: Defines the precise data structure of the weekly earnings aggregate record entity.

payload.statement: The aggregation service shall generate one weekly earnings record per child entity by computing the sum of task monetary values where task.status equals 'completed' and task.completion_timestamp is between week_start_date and week_end_date inclusive.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed algorithm for generating weekly earnings records within temporal boundaries.

payload.statement: The persistent storage layer shall retain task completion records with a minimum retention period extending beyond the current operational cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Enforces the retention constraint for historical task data beyond current cycle.

payload.statement: The persistent storage layer shall provide SQL query interface supporting SELECT operations on task completion records filtered by child_id, completion_timestamp, and status attributes.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed query access mechanism for historical task records.

payload.statement: The task definition API endpoint shall accept GET requests at path /api/tasks and return JSON response containing task_id, description, monetary_value (decimal(10,2)), and status fields for authorized users.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact API endpoint path, method, and response data structure for task definition access.

payload.statement: The task definition API endpoint shall enforce bearer token authentication and verify the token grants read:tasks permission before returning task definition records.
payload.rationale: Specifies the authorization mechanism for API endpoint access.

payload.statement: The child earnings API endpoint shall accept GET requests at path /api/child/earnings/{child_id}/weekly and return JSON response containing week_start_date, week_end_date, and total_earnings fields when the authenticated child_id matches the path parameter.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact API endpoint path, method, authorization scope, and response structure for child earnings retrieval.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that all task state transitions from 'available' to 'claimed', from 'claimed' to 'completed', and from 'completed' to 'available' occur only when the current_timestamp is within the same seven-day operational cycle window.
payload.rationale: Specifies the precise temporal constraint governing task lifecycle state transitions.

payload.statement: The task claiming API endpoint shall accept POST requests at path /api/tasks/{task_id}/claim with authenticated child role and return HTTP 200 with updated task record or HTTP 409 if task.status is not 'available'.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact REST API endpoint path, method, authentication requirement, and response codes for task claiming.

payload.statement: The task definition repository shall persist task records with status field initialized to 'available' and provide query method filterByStatusAndChildAuthorization(status, child_id) returning task records where status matches and task.parent_id equals child.parent_id.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed query method signature and authorization filter for child-scoped task access.

payload.statement: The task creation API endpoint shall accept POST requests at path /api/tasks with JSON body containing description (string, max 500 chars) and monetary_value (decimal(10,2), range 0.00-999.99) and return HTTP 201 with created task record for authenticated parent role.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact API endpoint path, method, request payload schema, field constraints, and response for task creation.

payload.statement: The task creation API endpoint authorization filter shall verify the bearer token contains role claim 'parent' and user_id matches a valid parent entity before permitting POST /api/tasks requests.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact role-based access control validation algorithm for task creation endpoint.

payload.statement: The task query API shall accept GET requests at path /api/tasks/available with authenticated child role and execute SQL query 'SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE status = available AND parent_id = (SELECT parent_id FROM children WHERE child_id = ?)'.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact API endpoint and SQL query implementation for state-filtered task retrieval with child authorization scope.

payload.statement: The available tasks API endpoint shall accept GET requests at path /api/tasks/available with authenticated child role and return JSON array of task objects where task.status equals 'available' and task is visible per child authorization scope.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact API endpoint path, method, role requirement, and filtered response for available task retrieval.

payload.statement: The aggregation service shall execute at the operational cycle endpoint event and compute weekly_earnings.total_earnings as SUM(task.monetary_value) WHERE task.child_id = weekly_earnings.child_id AND task.status = 'completed' AND task.completion_timestamp BETWEEN cycle_start AND cycle_end.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact aggregation calculation algorithm triggered by cycle endpoint event.

payload.statement: The parent reporting API endpoint shall accept GET requests at path /api/parent/reports/children/{parent_id} with authenticated parent role and return JSON containing array of child earnings aggregates and task completion records where child.parent_id equals path parameter.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact API endpoint path, method, authorization scope, and response structure for parent reporting access.

payload.statement: The reporting service API endpoint shall accept GET requests at path /api/reports/parent/{parent_id} with authenticated parent role and return JSON response containing child_earnings array and task_completions array aggregated by child_id within parent supervisory scope.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact reporting API endpoint path, method, and response data structure for parent-scoped child data.

payload.statement: The task claim handler shall execute SQL UPDATE tasks SET child_id = ?, claimed_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, status = 'claimed' WHERE task_id = ? AND status = 'available' when processing a task claimed event.
payload.rationale: Defines the exact SQL statement that establishes the foreign key relationship during task claiming operation.

payload.statement: The reporting service shall execute query 'SELECT child_id, SUM(monetary_value) as total, COUNT(*) as count FROM tasks WHERE status = completed AND parent_id = ? GROUP BY child_id' to aggregate completion data by child within parent supervisory scope.
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact SQL aggregation query for parent-scoped completion reporting grouped by child.

payload.statement: The system shall be deployed on a platform node running a supported operating system as specified in deployment configuration.
payload.rationale: Specifies the deployment target platform requirement at operational execution level.

payload.statement: The system shall execute only on operating system versions listed in the platform compatibility matrix (minimum: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS, Windows Server 2019, macOS 11.0).
payload.rationale: Specifies the exact operating system version constraints as implementable platform requirements.
payload.fit_criteria: System successfully deploys and executes on each listed OS version.
